headline: MY Bharat – Where Digital India Meets Young India
fullText:
PIB Backgrounder

MY Bharat – Where Digital India Meets Young India

Empowering Amrit Peedhi

05 JUL 2026 10:59AM by PIB Delhi

Youth at the Heart of Mera Yuva Bharat

India has the largest youth population in the world, with nearly 65% of its population below the age of 35. Recognising the immense potential of this demographic dividend, MY Bharat (Mera Yuva Bharat) was launched in October 2023 under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. The initiative aims to place youth at the centre of nation-building by providing opportunities for volunteering, skill development, leadership and civic participation.

MY Bharat serves as a digital platform that connects the Government, educational institutions, non-profit organisations and industry to create meaningful youth engagement. Enabled by India’s strong Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) under the broader Digital India initiative, the platform seamlessly connects millions of young Indians with learning and nation-building opportunities, ensuring greater inclusion, accessibility and participation across the country. Through this initiative, young citizens are being empowered to actively contribute towards the vision of Viksit Bharat@2047.

Empowering Amrit Peedhi Through Opportunities

MY Bharat serves as a unified digital platform by combining physical engagement with digital technology. The opportunities accessible through the portal are:

Digital Volunteerism

Over 1.52 lakh volunteer opportunities are available on the platform across cleanliness, blood donation, sports, disaster response, and community service, as of June 2026.

It offers end-to-end digital volunteer management through online registration, attendance, geo-tagging, certificate generation and impact tracking.

Experiential Learning Beyond Classrooms

As of July 2026, the platform offers over 24900+ Experiential Learning Programs (ELP), promoting hands-on learning and workforce readiness.

Additionally, opportunities also include internships with public and private organisations, apprenticeships for workplace training, and industry exposure.

The platform promotes civic leadership through quizzes and competitions that engage youth in understanding national issues like the Union Budget.

Leadership Development and Career Support

The platform offers leadership development through initiatives such as the Viksit Bharat Youth Parliament, enabling youth to debate national issues and build leadership skills.

AI-powered Resume builder and mentoring support helps youth prepare for their academic and career prospects.

Multilingual digital quizzes on governance, the Constitution, innovation and public policy, making learning interaction.

Initiatives Connecting Youth with the Nation

Youth-led Nation-building

MY Bharat MY Vote’ campaign promoted informed voting and constitutional awareness among first-time voters.

National Youth Festival: Viksit Bharat Young Leaders Dialogue 2026 witnessed participation of over 50.42 lakh youth, making it one of India’s largest youth consultation exercises.

The Viksit Bharat Young Leaders Dialogue (VBYLD) 2026 provided a national platform for youth to deliberate on ideas and solutions for Viksit Bharat@2047, encouraging leadership, innovation and meaningful participation in nation-building.

Youth for Viksit Bharat – MY Bharat Youth Convention 2026 brought together 6,000+ youth to celebrate the aspirations and achievements of the Amrit Peedhi.

The Nari Shakti Youth Parliament, conducted across 17 zones, engaged over 7,000 young women in structured democratic dialogue and policy discussions.

Volunteerism & Social Action

The National Service Scheme (NSS) leverages the MY Bharat platform to facilitate volunteering, participation and monitoring.

MY Bharat volunteers mobilised lakhs of youth during International Day of Yoga 2026 through yoga sessions across educational institutions, community spaces and iconic landmarks.

Through the Nasha Mukt Bharat campaign, youth volunteers support awareness drives, rehabilitation initiatives and community outreach for a drug-free India.

MY Bharat organises nationwide Padyatras, cleanliness drives and community service activities to strengthen civic participation and Jan Bhagidari.

Young Voices Beyond Borders

The Viksit Vibrant Village Programme enabled youth to experience life in India’s border villages, fostering national integration and grassroots engagement.

The MY Bharat Podcast Series, launched on 21 March 2026, features conversations with inspiring personalities and changemakers to promote active citizenship.

MY Bharat enabled India's youth participation at the United Nations Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) Youth Forum 2026. This provided young leaders with a platform to represent India's perspectives in global policy discussions.

MY Bharat Advancing Digital India's Vision

MY Bharat showcases how technology is making youth participation more accessible, recognised and future-ready across the country.

Participation and Recognition

MY Bharat earned a Guinness World Record title on 04 June 2026 for the "Most Users to Take an Online Quiz in One Week" with 390,812 verified participants.

The portal offers digital badges, certificates and verified participation records recognising volunteering, learning programmes and civic engagement.

Digital Governance in your Pocket

MY Bharat mobile application, available in 22 Indian languages, has recorded over 1 lakh downloads, as of 02 July 2026.

Single registration enabling seamless access to opportunities, learning resources, digital certificates and government initiatives across the country.

Future-ready Platform

MY Bharat 2.0 is transforming youth engagement through Artificial Intelligence (AI), multilingual technologies, open APIs and digital credentials to expand youth participation.

The platform represents India’s vision of empowering over 100 million youth through technology-driven governance aligned with Viksit Bharat@2047.

Building Future-ready Mera Yuva Bharat

Built on the foundations of Digital India, MY Bharat is empowering the Amrit Peedhi to participate in nation-building and drive India's journey towards a developed, inclusive and self-reliant nation.

As India advances towards Viksit Bharat@2047, MY Bharat is poised to become the country's largest Digital Public Infrastructure for youth. By leveraging Artificial Intelligence and multilingual technologies, the platform will continue to expand access to volunteering and experiential learning.

headline: India to Host BRICS Women Working Group Meeting in Kochi from 6-7 July 2026
fullText:
Ministry of Women and Child Development

India to Host BRICS Women Working Group Meeting in Kochi from 6-7 July 2026

Officials from BRICS Member Countries to advance cooperation and deliberate key outcome documents under India's BRICS Chairship

05 JUL 2026 10:11AM by PIB Delhi

India will host the BRICS Women Working Group (WWG) Meeting on 6-7 July 2026 in Kochi, Kerala, under its BRICS Chairship this year. Senior government officials from BRICS Member Countries will come together to deliberate on key issues relating to women's empowerment across all sectors and women-led development, while advancing cooperation across shared priorities.

Guided by India's BRICS Chairship theme, "Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability", reflecting a people-centric and humanity-first approach, the BRICS Women Track has focused on four priority areas: women in governance and leadership; women's digital and financial inclusion; women's entrepreneurship and skill development; and women's role in climate action, food security and nutrition.

The Women Working Group Meeting will provide an opportunity for Member Countries to deliberate and build consensus that advance women-led development.

BRICS comprises 11 countries - Brazil, China, Egypt, Ethiopia, India, Indonesia, Iran, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, and the United Arab Emirates, which together account for around 40 per cent of global GDP.

BRICS represents about 49.5% of the global population. Within this, women's political, social, and economic development shapes outcomes across every sector, making the Women Track a strategically vital track in BRICS 2026.

The Working Group Meeting builds upon the extensive consultations held through three virtual preparatory meetings convened virtually on 30 April 2026, 22 May 2026 and 29 June 2026. These engagements have enabled Member Countries to advance discussions ahead of the meetings in Kochi.

The BRICS Women Working Group Meeting is expected to lay a strong foundation for the BRICS Women Ministerial Meeting to be held on 8-9 July 2026, reaffirming the shared commitment of BRICS Member Countries towards practical cooperation and women-led development.

Under @BricsIndia2026 , Kochi will host the BRICS Women Working Group Meeting on 6–7 July 2026. The meeting will strengthen policy dialogue, deepen partnerships and advance the shared agenda of women-led development across BRICS Member Countries. #BRICS2026 @MEAIndia pic.twitter.com/E4oOViPFFL

— Ministry of WCD (@MinistryWCD) July 1, 2026

(Release ID: 2281194)

  1. Women in governance and leadership  
  2. Women’s digital and financial inclusion  
  3. Women’s entrepreneurship and skill development  
  4. Women’s role in climate action, food security, and nutrition  
- **BRICS composition:** 11 member countries – Brazil, China, Egypt, Ethiopia, India, Indonesia, Iran, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, and the United Arab Emirates.  
- **Economic and demographic weight:** BRICS accounts for approximately 40 % of global GDP and 49.5 % of the world population.  
- **Preparatory meetings:** Three virtual consultations were held on 30 April 2026, 22 May 2026, and 29 June 2026 to advance discussions ahead of the Kochi meeting.  
- **Link to Ministerial meeting:** The WWG meeting is expected to lay the groundwork for the BRICS Women Ministerial Meeting scheduled for 8–9 July 2026.  
- **Purpose:** To build consensus on women‑led development initiatives, deepen policy dialogue, and strengthen partnerships among BRICS member countries.

headline: Ministry of Culture to Organise a Special Commemorative Programme on the 125th Birth Anniversary of Bharat Kesari Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee in Kolkata tomorrow
fullText:
Ministry of Culture

Ministry of Culture to Organise a Special Commemorative Programme on the 125th Birth Anniversary of Bharat Kesari Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee in Kolkata tomorrow

Union Minister of Home Affairs and Cooperation Shri Amit Shah will grace the event as the Chief Guest

05 JUL 2026 7:01PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Culture, in association with the Government of West Bengal, will organise a special commemorative programme on tomorrow (6th July 2026) at Milan Mela Prangan, Kolkata, as part of the two year official commemoration of the 125th Birth Anniversary of Bharat Kesari Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee.

Union Minister of Home Affairs and Cooperation Shri Amit Shah will grace the programme as the Chief Guest. The event will also be attended by Union Minister of Culture and Tourism Shri Gajendra Singh Shekhawat, Chief Minister of West Bengal Shri Suvendu Adhikari, Chief Secretary, Government of West Bengal, Shri Manoj Agarwal, Shri Vivek Aggarwal, Secretary, Ministry of Culture, and other distinguished dignitaries.

The Ministry of Culture had announced a two-year nationwide commemoration of the 125th birth anniversary of Bharat Kesari Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee in 2025 to honour the enduring legacy of the visionary statesman, educationist, parliamentarian and nationalist leader whose contributions significantly shaped India’s political, cultural, educational and industrial landscape.

The day’s programme will commence with floral tributes by the Chief Guest Shri Amit Shah and other dignitaries at the residence of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee in Bhawanipore, Kolkata. Thereafter, the Chief Guest will visit Eco Park to perform the Bhoomi Poojan and lay the foundation stone for the proposed 125-foot statue of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee, a landmark initiative to honour his enduring contribution to the nation.

A special exhibition dedicated to the life and legacy of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee will also be inaugurated at Milan Mela Prangan. Through rare photographs, archival documents and immersive multimedia displays, the exhibition traces his personal journey, ideological vision, educational reforms, and his pivotal role in strengthening India’s democratic institutions and industrial development.

The commemorative programme at Milan Mela Prangan will feature the mass singing of Vande Mataram, followed by a grand musical tribute titled "Sur, Sanskriti evam Rashtra" by CCRT. The production will feature 57 accomplished artists representing diverse streams of Indian classical, folk and traditional music, reflecting India's rich cultural heritage and spirit of unity. Conceived as a musical narrative, the programme celebrates Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee's enduring ideals of cultural nationalism, national integration, education and civilisational values. The musical ensemble is directed by Padma Shri Tarun Bhattacharya.

The programme will also include the screening of a short film depicting the life and contributions of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee. The programme will also witness the release of a special video showcasing the foundation stone laying of the proposed 125-foot statue of Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee.

The two-year nationwide commemoration reflects the Government of India’s commitment to preserving and promoting the enduring legacy of one of the country’s foremost nation builders, inspiring present and future generations through his ideals of nationalism, public service, and unwavering dedication to India’s unity and progress.

(Release ID: 2281355)

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- The event, held at Milan Mela Prangan, Kolkata, is part of a two-year nationwide commemoration initiated in 2025 to honor Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee’s legacy.

headline: MSDE Extends Registration Deadline for Navachar Mantra to 15 July 2026
fullText:
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

MSDE Extends Registration Deadline for Navachar Mantra to 15 July 2026

Additional time to enable greater participation from aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators across the country

05 JUL 2026 6:56PM by PIB Delhi

Responding to the overwhelming interest from innovators across the country, the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) has extended the registration deadline for Navachar Mantra, its flagship national innovation platform, to 15 July 2026, providing more aspiring entrepreneurs the opportunity to transform promising ideas into successful ventures.

Thousands of registrations have already been received from aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators so far through the official Navachar Mantra registration portal. The extended registration window is expected to attract a greater number of registrations and bring forward many more innovative ideas from across India.

Launched under the aegis of the Ministry, Navachar Mantra is being implemented by the National Institute for Entrepreneurship and Small Business Development (NIESBUD), with the Foundation for Innovation and Technology Transfer (FITT), IIT Delhi, serving as the Knowledge Partner. The initiative seeks to identify, nurture and accelerate high-potential entrepreneurial ideas through structured mentorship, expert guidance, industry connect and market access.

Designed as a comprehensive platform for innovation-led entrepreneurship, Navachar Mantra offers participants access to expert mentoring, incubation support, industry partnerships, investor connect and institutional handholding. The initiative aims to help innovators refine, validate and scale their ideas into sustainable enterprises with real market potential, while creating an enabling ecosystem for entrepreneurship across the country.

Conceived to bridge the gap between innovation and enterprise, Navachar Mantra aligns with the Government's vision of fostering innovation-driven growth, nurturing job creators and strengthening India's startup ecosystem. By encouraging participation from students, startups, professionals and grassroots innovators—including those from Tier-II, Tier-III and rural India—the initiative seeks to unlock entrepreneurial potential from every corner of the country.

The extension of the registration deadline reflects the Ministry's commitment to ensuring that deserving innovators have adequate time to participate in the programme and benefit from its extensive support ecosystem. It also reinforces MSDE's efforts to make innovation and entrepreneurship more inclusive, accessible and representative of India's diverse talent pool.

The Ministry has encouraged students, startups, professionals, grassroots innovators and aspiring entrepreneurs from across the country to make the most of this extended opportunity and take the first step towards transforming their ideas into impactful enterprises.

Explore More: https://nm.fitt-iitd.in/

Apply Here: https://accubate.fitt-iitd.in/ext/form/23520/1/apply?source=Print&medium=post

headline: Union Home and Cooperation Minister Shri Amit Shah to lay the Foundation Stone and Inaugurate Several Key Initiatives at the 5th Foundation Day Ceremony of the Ministry of Cooperation in New Delhi Tomorrow
fullText:
Ministry of Cooperation

Union Home and Cooperation Minister Shri Amit Shah to lay the Foundation Stone and Inaugurate Several Key Initiatives at the 5th Foundation Day Ceremony of the Ministry of Cooperation in New Delhi Tomorrow

Transfer, Inauguration and Foundation Stone Laying of 232 Grain Storage Godowns under the World's Largest Grain Storage Scheme

Bhoomi Pujan of ‘Sahakar Van’ a 64 Acre cooperative forestation project by Amul and NCCF

Bhoomi Pujan of tissue culture facilities of BBSSL in Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra

Launch of NCD 3.0 and geo-tag mobile application and NDDB’s Milk Supply Review Dashboard Portal

50,000 PACS to be transformed into e-PACS

Shri Amit shah to unveil two major digital initiatives of NUCFDC — Sahakar CBS and Sahakar Sahyogi for Urban Cooperative Banks

Inauguration of Cooperative Milk Producers Organisation Multi-State Limited and Gomay Cooperative Society Multi-State Limited

MoU to be signed between BBSSL and ICAR to strengthen seed systems; model bye-laws for dairy cooperative societies to be released

05 JUL 2026 5:21PM by PIB Delhi

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah will attend the 5th Foundation Day Ceremony of the Ministry of Cooperation at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, tomorrow, 6 July 2026. The ceremony will be held in line with the vision of “Sahakar Se Samriddhi” of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and under the leadership of Shri Amit Shah, who has guided the cooperative sector towards greater transparency, technology adoption, institutional strengthening and grassroots empowerment. In the last five years, the Ministry has undertaken several transformative initiatives for Primary Agricultural Credit Societies, cooperative banks, dairy cooperatives, urban cooperatives, national-level cooperative institutions and multi-state cooperative societies.

Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh, Union Minister of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying and Panchayati Raj; Shri Bhajan Lal Sharma, Chief Minister of Rajasthan; Shri Krishan Pal Gurjar, Minister of State for Cooperation; and Shri Murlidhar Mohol, Minister of State for Cooperation and Civil Aviation, will also grace the occasion. Senior officers of various Ministries including; Dr. Ashish Kumar Bhutani, Secretary, Ministry of Cooperation; Shri Atish Chandra, Secretary, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare; and Shri Naresh Pal Gangwar, Secretary, Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, will also be present. Representatives of national cooperative institutions, cooperative federations, cooperative banks, dairy cooperatives, Bharat Taxi, PACS and other stakeholders from the cooperative sector will also attend the programme.

Shri Amit Shah will also lay the Foundation Stone and Inaugurate several major projects and initiatives on this occasion. These include the transfer of 135 godowns, the inauguration of 85 godowns, and the laying of the foundation stone for 47 grain storage godowns; Bhoomi Pujan of Sahakar Van by Amul and NCCF, Bhoomi Pujan of tissue culture facilities of BBSSL at Barabanki in Uttar Pradesh and Jalgaon in Maharashtra, launch of NCD 3.0 and geo-tag mobile application; launch of NDDB’s Milk Supply Review Dashboard Portal, initiatives related to productivity of dairy animals under Cooperative Inputs and Services Delivery Multi-State Limited, inauguration of Cooperative Milk Producers Organisation Multi-State Limited, inauguration of Gomay Cooperative Society Multi-State Limited; and unveiling of two major initiatives of NUCFDC — Sahakar CBS, a centralised core banking platform, and Sahakar Sahyogi, a conversational AI-powered platform for Urban Cooperative Banks.

Another major highlight of the programme will be the transformation of 50,000 PACS into e-PACS, marking a significant step towards digital empowerment of grassroots cooperative institutions.

During the programme, a Memorandum of Understanding will also be signed between BBSSL and ICAR for strengthening seed systems. On this occasion, Shri Amit Shah will release model bye-laws for dairy cooperative societies and a book highlighting the achievements of the Ministry of Cooperation during the last five years.

The ceremony will reaffirm the Ministry’s commitment to building a cooperative ecosystem that connects farmers, women, youth, small producers, dairy farmers, urban cooperatives and rural institutions with the vision of “Sahakar Se Samriddhi” and of a developed and self-reliant India.

The successful completion of the visit marks another milestone in the growing maritime partnership between India and Singapore. The engagements undertaken during the port call have contributed towards enhancing professional understanding, strengthening operational cooperation and promoting shared efforts towards maintaining peace, stability and security in the region.

headline: Senior Officers of the Ministry of Rural Development to Visit to Karnataka for Review of Rural Development Programmes including lunch of Viksit Bharat-GRAMG
fullText:
Ministry of Rural Development

Senior Officers of the Ministry of Rural Development to Visit to Karnataka for Review of Rural Development Programmes including lunch of Viksit Bharat-GRAMG

05 JUL 2026 4:52PM by PIB Delhi

As part of the Ministry of Rural Development's ongoing Nefforts to strengthen the implementation of flagship rural development programmes, a team of senior officers from the Department of Rural Development, Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India, comprising Shri Altaf Hussain Haji (ISS) Deputy Director General & Statistical Advisor DORD along with Shri Anil Kumar K.C, Under Secretary and Shri Baldev Verma, Assistant Director will undertake an official visit to Karnataka from 5th to 11th July, 2026.

The visit is aimed at providing handholding, monitoring and implementation support to the Government of Karnataka for effective execution of rural development programmes. During the visit, the officers will hold review meetings with State Government officials in Bengaluru to assess the progress of key schemes and discuss measures for improving programme implementation and service delivery.

The team will also visit Belgavi and Davanagere districts to support the rollout of VB- GRAMG and review its implementation at the field level. The officers will assess the preparedness of district administrations, identify implementation challenges and facilitate timely resolution of issues to ensure effective rollout of the initiative.

In addition to VB-GRAMG, the officers will review the implementation of other flagship programmes of the Ministry of Rural Development, including Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana–Gramin (PMAY-G), Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana–National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM), Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS) and the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP).

The field visits will include interactions with district officials, Panchayati Raj Institution representatives, Self-Help Group (SHG) members, existing MGNREGS job card holders and beneficiaries of various rural development schemes. These interactions are intended to obtain first-hand feedback on programme implementation, understand field-level challenges, document best practices and identify opportunities for further improving service delivery.

The Ministry of Rural Development is committed to ensuring transparent, accountable and effective implementation of its flagship programmes through continuous monitoring, close coordination with State Governments and regular field engagements. Such visits strengthen cooperative federalism and contribute to achieving the objective of inclusive and sustainable rural development across the country.

headline: Government secures future of nearly 65,000 erstwhile Ordnance Factory employees through redeployment into Regular Government Service: Dr. Jitendra Singh
fullText:
Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions

Government secures future of nearly 65,000 erstwhile Ordnance Factory employees through redeployment into Regular Government Service: Dr. Jitendra Singh

19-member Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh-led delegation thanks Prime Minister Narendra Modi-led Government for employee-centric reforms

Government pursuing reforms to strengthen employee welfare and administrative efficiency: Dr. Jitendra Singh

05 JUL 2026 3:41PM by PIB Delhi

A 19-member delegation representing Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh (BMS) and affiliated employees' organisations from across the country called on Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences and Minister of State for PMO, Department of Atomic Energy, Department of Space, Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Dr. Jitendra Singh, today to discuss a range of issues relating to Government employees, pensioners and administrative reforms.

The delegation appreciated the Minister for facilitating the Government's employee-centric initiatives, particularly the decision to redeploy nearly 65,000 erstwhile Ordnance Factory employees into regular Government service, describing it as a landmark measure that has secured the future of thousands of employees following the restructuring of the Ordnance Factory Board.

The delegation comprised representatives of Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh (BMS), Bharatiya Pratiraksha Mazdoor Sangh (BPMS), Government Employees National Confederation (GENC), Public Sector Employees National Confederation, SHAR Employees Trade Union and Space Employees Association, representing employees from defence establishments, public sector organisations, scientific institutions and other Central Government services across the country.

Welcoming the delegation, Dr. Jitendra Singh appreciated the constructive role played by employee organisations in bringing service-related issues before the Government through institutional dialogue. He said regular interaction with stakeholders helps strengthen governance and facilitates timely resolution of issues concerning Government employees and pensioners.

The delegation thanked Dr. Jitendra Singh, as a member of the Empowered Group of Ministers (EGoM), for supporting the extension of deemed deputation of erstwhile Ordnance Factory employees till the date of retirement. Representatives said the subsequent decision to redeploy these employees into regular Government service has provided long-term service security to nearly 65,000 employees and addressed one of the most significant concerns arising from the corporatisation of the Ordnance Factory Board.

The representatives also discussed a number of service-related issues requiring further consideration. These included extending the benefit of the Old Pension Scheme to compassionate appointment cases, career progression, cadre-related matters and wider stakeholder consultations on issues concerning Government employees.

The delegation also appreciated the continued engagement with recognised employee organisations during deliberations on matters relating to personnel administration and future policy reforms. It suggested that administrative processes may continue to be simplified to reduce avoidable litigation and ensure timely resolution of service-related matters affecting employees.

Dr. Jitendra Singh said, under PM Sh Narendra Modi, the Government remains committed to a consultative and responsive approach in addressing issues concerning Government employees and pensioners. He said every genuine representation receives due consideration through the appropriate Ministries and Departments and that suggestions aimed at improving governance, simplifying procedures and enhancing administrative efficiency are always welcome.

The Minister said, PM Modi has always prioritised making governance more transparent, efficient and citizen-centric through progressive administrative reforms. He said continued dialogue with employee organisations plays an important role in identifying practical issues and evolving appropriate policy responses in the larger public interest.

The delegation expressed confidence that the Government would continue to pursue employee-friendly reforms while maintaining regular engagement with representative organisations on matters relating to service conditions, pension administration and personnel management.

The members thanked Dr. Jitendra Singh for the opportunity to present their views and appreciated the Government's consultative approach towards addressing issues concerning Government employees.

headline: Ethanol Blending in India
fullText:
PIB Backgrounder

Ethanol Blending in India

Policy Evolution, Key Milestones and Frequently Raised Concerns

05 JUL 2026 3:33PM by PIB Delhi

Overview of the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme

Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme has emerged as a key pillar of India's energy transition and biofuel strategy. It aims to improve energy security, support farmers, and reduce environmental impacts. The programme also promotes greater use of domestically produced renewable fuel.

Under the EBP Programme, ethanol blending has increased from less than 1.5 percent in 2013-14 to 20 percent in 2025-26. India achieved the 20 percent blending target five years ahead of schedule. Ethanol procurement rose from about 38 crore litres in Ethanol Supply Year (ESY) 2013-14 to over 1,200 crore litres (projected) in 2025-26. Production capacity expanded nearly fivefold from 421 crore litres in 2014 to about 2,000 crore litres in 2026. This expansion has reduced crude oil imports and saved valuable foreign exchange. It has also lowered greenhouse gas emissions and strengthened farmers' incomes through new market opportunities.

Strengthening Energy Security through Ethanol Blending

India imports close to 88.5 percent of the crude oil it consumes. That single number explains a great deal about why ethanol blending sits so high on the policy agenda. Every barrel of oil bought from abroad exposes the country to price swings and supply shocks that are entirely outside its control. Ethanol produced from Indian sugarcane, maize and rice offers a way to blunt that exposure using resources grown at home.

Since the ESY 2014-15 (up to May 2026), the programme has delivered results that go well beyond a policy target on paper.

₹1.90+ lakh crore Saved in foreign exchange since 2014-15 | 310+ lakh MT* Crude oil substituted | ~930 lakh MT* CO2 emissions cut | ₹1.60+ lakh crore Additional farmer earnings

*MT – Metric Tonnes

Setting the Record Straight

Here is what the evidence actually shows on the key concerns surrounding ethanol blending, along with the more colourful myths that have circulated on social media.

The Claim | The Record

E20 cuts mileage by 30 percent. | The 30 percent figure refers only to ethanol's lower calorific value compared to petrol, not to a drop-in real-world mileage. Mileage depends on driving habits, tyre pressure, servicing and AC load far more than fuel type.

E20 damages engines, especially older ones. | No widespread pattern of engine failure linked to E20 has been reported since rollout. E20 was cleared only after extensive testing by SIAM, ARAI and IOCL together with vehicle makers.

Ethanol is not a high-performance fuel and reduces vehicle performance. | Ethanol is a high-octane fuel, with a research octane number of about 108.5, compared to 84.4 for petrol. E20 raises the effective octane rating of Indian petrol to around 95, improving combustion in modern engines. Vehicles calibrated for E20 can deliver better acceleration, smoother performance, and lower emissions.

Insurance companies refuse claims for E20 related damage. | Insurers and OEMs have clarified: E20 has NO impact on insurance or warranty validity in India. SIAM has categorically confirmed warranties will be honoured for vehicles running spec-compliant E20.

Ethanol blended fuel should be cheaper, and government is pocketing the difference. | The NITI Aayog report cited for this claim is from 2020-21, when ethanol was indeed cheaper than petrol. Ethanol procurement costs have since risen above refined petrol costs, yet the mandate continues because of the energy security, environmental and farm income gains it delivers.

The Government told the Supreme Court that E20 is just an 'experiment'. | The case was about ethanol procurement contracts -not E20's merits. The Attorney General's office clarified on 30.06.2026 that the 'experiment' claim is incorrect.

Sugarcane juice is literally poured into petrol. | Viral videos showing raw juice mixed with petrol are misleading. Ethanol is produced through fermentation and industrial processing that changes its properties entirely, and it must meet strict fuel quality specifications before blending.

1 litre of ethanol consumes 10,000 litres of water. | An ethanol plant uses only 3-5 litres of processed water per litre of ethanol. Modern distilleries run Zero Liquid Discharge systems. Attributing the entire agricultural water footprint of paddy cultivation to ethanol production is incorrect. Only surplus rice, as determined and permitted by the DFPD after meeting national food security requirements, is diverted for ethanol production.

Ants and bees swarm fuel caps because E20 contains sugar. | Fuel ethanol is distilled - residual sugars are eliminated. It contains insect-repellent denaturants, petrol's odour dominates, and E20 forms less vapour than regular petrol.

Ethanol's water absorbing nature will spoil fuel and ruin tanks. | Keeping water out of any fuel tank, ethanol blended or not, is basic vehicle design. Modern vehicles are equipped with design features and safeguards to prevent water entry into fuel tanks.

Industry Voices of Confidence

Confidence in E20 is reinforced by leading automobile manufacturers and energy sector experts, drawing on extensive testing and real-world service data.

Some of the key observations from industry leaders are as follows:

Vikram Gulati, Country Head and Executive Vice President, Toyota Kirloskar Motor: Vehicles undergo rigorous, independent certification worldwide. Ethanol is a proven, high-performance fuel, in use since the early 1900s. Decision to move to E20 was taken only after rigorous testing on older vehicles

Rahul Bharti, Senior Executive Officer, Corporate Affairs, Maruti Suzuki: E10-designed vehicles have been tested extensively with E20 fuel. Across 2.84 crore vehicles serviced by Maruti Suzuki in FY 2025-26, more than 1.5 crore were over three years old and hence not E20-certified. Still no E20-related damage was found. On mileage, the real impact is small. For a car giving 20 km per litre, the drop is roughly 0.6 km. Driving habits and maintenance affect mileage far more than fuel type. Better acceleration and lower pollution offset this small difference.

Ashutosh Varma, Chief Business Officer, Hero MotoCorp: Analysis of extensive service data found no incidence of higher damage in vehicles running on E20 compared to earlier fuels.

Vartika Shukla, Former Chairman and Managing Director, Engineers India Limited: The ethanol blending programme was developed through extensive consultation with all stakeholders. It is supported by scientific evidence, rigorous automotive testing, and global best practices. E20 fuel meets BIS standards and BS-VI emission norms. It is available uniformly across retail outlets nationwide.

Across manufacturing and energy sectors, the message from industry leaders is consistent. E20 is safe, well-tested, and backed by real-world data.

Ethanol Blending Is a Globally Proven Fuel Strategy

India is far from alone in adopting ethanol blending. It is now a globally accepted practice. Several major economies have built ethanol firmly into their fuel strategy.

United States: E10 is the standard ethanol blended fuel nationwide. E15 is expanding rapidly, backed by the US government. Millions of vehicles are already flex fuel capable. These can run on blends as high as E85.

Brazil: Brazil remains the global leader in ethanol use. It currently mandates E27 as its standard petrol blend. This is being raised further to around 35 percent. Over 80 percent of new cars sold are flex fuel vehicles. These run on E27, E30, or pure hydrous ethanol.

Japan: Japan has brought ethanol into its fuel mix too. This was done through a phased E10 rollout.

Countries including Canada, Thailand, and several European nations have also adopted ethanol blending as part of their clean fuel strategies.

Powering India's Energy Future

The Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme has evolved into a key pillar of India's energy strategy. Its progress reflects scientific evaluation, phased implementation, and collaboration across government, industry, and research institutions. The programme has strengthened energy security, reduced emissions, and created new income opportunities for farmers. It has also reduced crude oil imports and promoted the use of domestically produced renewable fuel. The EBP Programme will continue contributing to a cleaner, more resilient, and self-reliant transport fuel ecosystem.

### OVERVIEW  
India's Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme has expanded ethanol blending from under 1.5% in 2013-14 to 20% in 2025-26, achieving the target five years ahead of schedule to enhance energy security, environmental sustainability, and agricultural support.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- Ethanol blending increased from less than 1.5% (2013-14) to 20% (2025-26), surpassing targets.  
- Ethanol procurement grew from approximately 38 crore litres in 2013-14 to over 1,200 crore litres (projected for 2025-26).  
- Production capacity expanded nearly fivefold, from 421 crore litres (2014) to about 2,000 crore litres (2026).  
- Foreign exchange savings estimated at ₹1.90+ lakh crore since 2014-15 due to reduced crude oil imports.  
- Crude oil substitution of ~310+ lakh metric tonnes (MT) since 2014-15.  
- Greenhouse gas emissions reduced by ~930 lakh MT since programme inception.  
- Additional farmer earnings of ₹1.60+ lakh crore linked to ethanol production.  
- Ethanol blending achieved in 2025-26 five years ahead of schedule.  
- Industry endorsements from Toyota Kirloskar Motor, Maruti Suzuki, Hero MotoCorp, and Engineers India Limited affirm program viability.

headline: Union Minister of Youth Affairs & Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya leads nationwide 80th edition of Fit India Sundays on Cycle celebrating 100 years of Sri Sathya Sai
fullText:
Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

Union Minister of Youth Affairs & Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya leads nationwide 80th edition of Fit India Sundays on Cycle celebrating 100 years of Sri Sathya Sai

"One out of 4 persons in India is overweight. Fitness is essential for all citizens": Dr Mansukh Mandaviya

"I would urge all Indians to set aside one hour from their mobile screens and dedicate that time to their personal health by engaging in sports activities": Union Minister Dr Mandaviya

05 JUL 2026 2:44PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister of Youth Affairs & Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya led the nationwide 80th edition of Fit India Sundays on Cycle today from the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in New Delhi.

Sunday sorted. Two wheels, one goal: a fitter India. 🚴#SundaysOnCycle

📍 JLN Stadium, New Delhi pic.twitter.com/wSNL8UvtV2

— Dr Mansukh Mandaviya (@mansukhmandviya) July 5, 2026

This edition was organised in collaboration with Sri Sathya Sai Seva Organisations, celebrating the 100 years of spiritual leader Sri Sathya Sai.

The National Capital saw over 9,000 individuals participate in a community run and ride, apart from Zumba and Yoga sessions. Elsewhere, the cycling drive was organised in association with Art of Living, Heartfulness and Isha Foundation.

A dedicated Game Zone featuring life-sized chess and ludo, along with carrom, table tennis, futsal and badminton, was also set up to engage children.

Reinforcing the vision of Hon'ble Prime Minister Narendra Modi of a Viksit Bharat in 2047, Dr Mandaviya said, “For any country to be counted as a developed nation, the health of its citizens is essential, I would urge all Indians to set aside at least one hour from their mobile screens and dedicate that time to their personal health by engaging in sports activities”.

Addressing the gathering, The Union Minister further said, "One out of 4 persons in India is overweight which makes fitness essential for all citizens”.

The community fitness ride began with Vedic invocations and lamp lighting by Dr Mandaviya along with Nimish Gulabrai Pandya, All India President of Sri Sathya Sai Seva Organisation, Shri Hari Ranjan Rao, Secretary (Sports), Deepti Mukherjee, Secretary (Corporate Affairs) and other dignitaries. This was followed by a public pledge of Nasha Mukt Bharat before the community fitness ride began.

Speaking on the occasion, Pandya said, "Fit India's motto is to make every Indian citizen fit and healthy while Sri Sathya Sai Seva Organisation's motto is to love all, serve all. This coming together of both institutions is an excellent amalgamation”. Noted actor Naseer Abdullah also attended the event and appreciated the initiative.

Started in December 2024 by Dr Mandaviya, Fit India Sundays on Cycle has grown into a mass movement encouraging citizens of all age groups to dedicate "fitness ki dose, aadha ghanta roz" (dedicate 30 minutes every day to become fit). Till now, more than 30 lakh people have participated in the event at thousands of locations across the country.

The event is organised by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports (MYAS) in collaboration with the Cycling Federation of India (CFI), Yogasana Bharat, Raahgiri Foundation and MY Bharat. The cycling drive is conducted simultaneously across all States and Union Territories, including SAI Regional Centres, National Centres of Excellence (NCOEs), SAI Training Centres (STCs), Khelo India State Centres of Excellence (KISCEs) and Khelo India Centres (KICs).

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
- **Event**: 80th edition of Fit India Sundays on Cycle held on July 5, 2026, from JLN Stadium, New Delhi.

- **Key Statements**:

  - Urged Indians to dedicate one hour daily from mobile screens to sports for a "Viksit Bharat" by 2047.  
- **Initiatives**: Included a public pledge of Nasha Mukt Bharat (drug-free India) and Vedic invocations at the start.  
- **Organizers**: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports (MYAS) in partnership with Cycling Federation of India, Yogasana Bharat, Raahgiri Foundation, and MY Bharat.  
- **Scope**: Conducted across all States, Union Territories, SAI Regional Centres, NCOEs, STCs, KISCEs, and KICs.
